steps.title: Compare the two models
steps.content: Text 1 says Ramanathan et al. identified K2-18 b as a hycean candidate on the inner edge of the habitable zone at about 1 AU. Text 2 says Chen et al. recalculated that inner edge for hydrogen-rich hycean candidates as far out as 3.85 AU.

steps.title: Infer Chen's response
steps.content: If the inner edge is much farther out, then a planet at about 1 AU would be too close to its star for surface liquid water. Chen et al. would therefore challenge Ramanathan et al.'s candidate by pointing to the effect of its hydrogen-rich atmosphere: higher surface temperatures and increased evaporation.

steps.title: Eliminate the reversals
steps.content: Choice A says the planets are too far from their stars, but Chen's model implies K2-18 b is too close. Choice C reverses the temperature claim because Chen et al. find higher, not lower, temperatures. Choice D adds an unsupported comparison to other planet types. The answer is B.
